attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who identifies suitable stages early and commits to early moves. Shows fast early pace in time trial efforts but vulnerable to negative splitters.
team_tactics: Limited team support visible; operates independently in breakaways, relying on personal stage reading and timing rather than coordinated team setups.
terrain_preference: Breakaway-friendly stages with reduced GC contender interest. Exposed in pure time trial disciplines where pacing strategy and power reserves become critical over sustained efforts.
vulnerability: Time trial pacing: starts too fast and fades in final sections, losing significant time (30+ seconds) to riders executing negative splits. Struggles when opponents accelerate through finish.
key_patterns: Correctly identifies breakaway-suitable stages multiple times within same Grand Tour (2 observations).
Aggressive positive-split pacing strategy in time trials, too fast early then fading (2 observations).
Loses time to negative-split TT specialists in final sections despite strong starts (2 observations).
form_trajectory: neutral: Shows consistent breakaway reading ability and stage-winning capacity, but recurring TT pacing weaknesses suggest tactical limitations rather than form decline.
